codeReview.summary: The feature handoff does not match the assigned editor-popover click-interception fix. Commit df33d5a only changes a timer-page week-range selector and contains no scoped editor-popover implementation, so the original blocking regression is not actually addressed by this feature evidence.

issues.description: Commit df33d5a only updates the timer-page week-range button selector. It does not touch the assigned editor popover path in `apps/website/e2e/time-entry-editor.real-runtime.spec.ts` or the editor UI code, so this feature does not implement the requested `Edit start time` click-interception fix.

The assigned failing interaction remains unchanged in this feature commit and the handoff attributes success to an unrelated selector repair. Passing reruns are not sufficient evidence for a flaky-fix feature when the cited commit contains no stabilization for the editor-popover interaction.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `frontend-test-worker` guidance still allowed a rerun-only handoff for a flaky-fix feature even though the cited commit did not contain the scoped stabilization. This feature repeated the previously observed traceability gap: the worker claimed the editor-popover fix was complete while landing an unrelated timer-page selector change.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/frontend-test-worker/SKILL.md` says the handoff commit must directly contain the scoped fix, but transcript session `add63759-b8fa-4477-9812-a17a91cb18c0` ends with commit `df33d5a`; `git show --stat df33d5a` changes only `apps/website/e2e/timer-page.spec.ts` while the assigned feature targets `apps/website/e2e/time-entry-editor.real-runtime.spec.ts`.

summary: Fail: the reported fix is untraceable to the assigned editor-popover regression. Commit `df33d5a` only changes the timer-page week-range selector and provides no scoped implementation for the `Edit start time` click-interception bug.
